role: MP for Tehran, 12th Majlis (2024–present); member, Front of Islamic Revolution Stability
bio: Mahmoud Nabavian is a Shia cleric who topped the Tehran ballot in the 2024 parliamentary election with the highest vote count in the capital. A student of [Mohammad-Taqi Mesbah Yazdi](/people/mohammad-taqi-mesbah-yazdi) and a senior member of the [Front of Islamic Revolution Stability](/orgs/front-of-islamic-revolution-stability), he has been a fierce opponent of Iran's nuclear diplomacy, comparing the [JCPOA](/events/jcpoa-nuclear-deal-2015) to Iran's humiliating 1828 territorial concessions to Russia. He attacked [Mohammad Javad Zarif](/people/javad-zarif) over the nuclear deal and was accused of lobbying against its revival in Vienna in 2022. He holds a PhD in philosophy from the [Imam Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) Educational and Research Institute. Previously served in the 9th [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) (2012–2016).
editorial_notes: Birth year not publicly confirmed. Top vote-getter in Tehran, March 2024. Also served 9th Majlis (2012–2016).
